metadata.title: WHO Guidelines on Nonclinical Evaluation of Vaccines — Annex 1

rules.rule_text: Vaccine production must use a seed-lot system; the biological nature of the vaccine must be fully characterized prior to nonclinical evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccine production should use a seed-lot system. The biological nature of the vaccine should be fully characterized prior to nonclinical evaluation.

rules.rule_text: At minimum, GMP compliance is required for vaccine clinical trial material; full GMP compliance must be achieved before licensure.
rules.verbatim_quote: At minimum, GMP compliance is required for clinical trial material. Full GMP compliance is required before licensure.
rules.exceptions: Reduced GMP requirements acceptable for early clinical trials only
cross_references: WHO GMP Guidelines for Biological Products

rules.rule_text: Consistency of production is crucial for vaccines; any manufacturing changes require a comparability exercise to demonstrate that the change does not adversely affect quality, safety, or efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Consistency of production is crucial for vaccines. Manufacturing changes require a comparability exercise to demonstrate that the change does not adversely affect quality, safety, or efficacy.

rules.rule_text: Licensed procedures for established vaccines may not apply to novel vaccines; manufacturers of novel vaccines should consult with the NRA on applicable requirements.
rules.verbatim_quote: Licensed procedures for established vaccines may not apply to novel vaccines. Manufacturers of novel vaccines should consult with the NRA on applicable requirements.
rules.exceptions: Applies to novel vaccine platforms and technologies

rules.rule_text: Potency of the candidate vaccine must be assessed through measurement of biological activity; for vectored vaccines expressing an insert, CFU or infectious titre testing of the vector must be supplemented with expression insert characterization.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potency should be assessed through biological activity. For vectored vaccines, CFU or infectious titre testing of the vector should be supplemented with expression insert characterization.
rules.exceptions: Additional requirements apply to vectored vaccines

rules.rule_text: Stability data must be sufficient to support the duration of preclinical and clinical trials; long-term real-time stability studies are required for licensure.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability data should be sufficient to support the duration of preclinical and clinical trials. Long-term real-time stability studies are required for licensure.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Potency must be included as a critical attribute in stability testing programmes for vaccine candidates.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potency should be included as a critical attribute in stability testing programmes.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: For novel vaccine products, the NRA must be consulted to agree on applicable international and national guidelines before commencing nonclinical development.
rules.verbatim_quote: For novel products, the NRA should be consulted to agree on applicable international and national guidelines.

rules.rule_text: Independent batch release by the NRA or national control laboratory (NCL) must be performed before market release of each lot; validation of lot release tests must be finalized prior to licensure.
rules.source_section: 2.5 Batch release and independent laboratory evaluation
rules.verbatim_quote: Independent batch release by the NRA or NCL should be performed before market release. Validation of lot release tests should be finalized prior to licensure.
rules.exceptions: NRA samples are required in some countries before any lot is released
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on national regulatory authorities

rules.rule_text: WHO International Biological Standards serve as primary standards worldwide; secondary standards must be calibrated against these International Standards.
rules.verbatim_quote: WHO International Biological Standards serve as primary standards worldwide. Secondary standards should be calibrated against these International Standards.

rules.rule_text: Immunogenicity studies must assess both humoral and/or cell-mediated immunity, and must include a functional immune response measure such as neutralizing antibodies or opsonophagocytic activity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity studies should assess both humoral and/or cell-mediated immunity, and should include a functional immune response measure such as neutralizing antibodies or opsonophagocytic activity.

rules.rule_text: Challenge/protection studies in animals must be performed to confirm relevance of the animal model when such models are available.
rules.verbatim_quote: Challenge/protection studies in animals should be performed to confirm relevance of the animal model when such models are available.
rules.exceptions: Applies when a relevant animal challenge model exists

rules.rule_text: Toxicity study design must be adequate to identify and characterize toxic effects; the route of administration must match the intended clinical route, and the same delivery device must be used if the vaccine is administered by device.
rules.verbatim_quote: Study design should be adequate to identify and characterize toxic effects. The route of administration should match the intended clinical route, and the same device should be used if the vaccine is given by device.

rules.rule_text: A reversibility group must be included in the toxicity study design to assess the reversibility of any observed toxic effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: A reversibility group should be included in the study design to assess the reversibility of toxic effects.

rules.rule_text: Outbred animal species are recommended for toxicity studies; one relevant species is sufficient for initial studies; rodents should be approximately 10 males and 10 females per group.
rules.verbatim_quote: Outbred animal species are recommended for toxicity studies. One relevant species is sufficient for initiation. Rodents should be approximately 10 males and 10 females per group.
rules.exceptions: Additional species may be required depending on vaccine type

rules.rule_text: Animal age at study initiation should be 6–8 weeks for rodents and 3–4 months for rabbits to reflect immunological maturity appropriate for the intended clinical population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Animal age at study initiation should be 6–8 weeks for rodents and 3–4 months for rabbits.
rules.exceptions: Different ages may be appropriate for pediatric vaccine programs

rules.rule_text: Dosing for toxicity studies should maximize exposure and immune response; the highest clinically intended dose should be used if feasible, and the number of doses must be at least equal to the number planned in humans.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dosing should maximize exposure and immune response. The highest clinically intended dose should be used if feasible, and the number of doses should be at least equal to the number planned in humans.
rules.exceptions: Dose-response is not required for basic toxicity assessment

rules.rule_text: A negative control group is mandatory in all vaccine toxicity studies; an active control group may also be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: A negative control group is mandatory in all toxicity studies. An active control group may also be included.

rules.rule_text: Parameters monitored in toxicity studies must include: daily clinical observations, weekly body weights and food consumption, haematology and serum chemistry at 1–3 days after the first and last dose and at the end of the recovery period, complete gross necropsy, and histopathological examination of immune organs, pivotal organs, and injection sites.
rules.verbatim_quote: Parameters should include daily clinical observations, weekly body weights and food consumption, haematology and serum chemistry 1–3 days after first and last dose and at recovery period end, complete gross necropsy, and histopathological examination of immune organs, pivotal organs, and injection site.

rules.rule_text: Local tolerance assessment must be included as part of the repeated-dose toxicity study or conducted as a standalone study, and must evaluate all contact sites and any inadvertently exposed sites.
rules.verbatim_quote: Local tolerance should be assessed as part of the repeated-dose toxicity study or as a standalone study, evaluating all contact sites and inadvertently exposed sites.

rules.rule_text: Special immunological investigations must be conducted to assess autoimmune potential, molecular mimicry risks, and hypersensitivity to antigen, adjuvant, excipients, and preservatives.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special immunological investigations should be conducted to assess autoimmune potential, molecular mimicry, and hypersensitivity to antigen, adjuvant, excipients, and preservatives.

rules.rule_text: Developmental toxicity studies are not necessary for childhood vaccines; however, they are required if the target population includes women of childbearing potential unless a scientifically justified exception is provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity studies are not necessary for childhood vaccines but are required if the target population includes women of childbearing potential unless a scientific justification is provided.
rules.exceptions: Childhood vaccines are exempt; scientific justification may provide an alternative path

rules.rule_text: Developmental toxicity studies must include prenatal and postnatal developmental assessment; the gestating animal must be exposed from implantation to closure of the hard palate/end of gestation (stages C, D, E per ICH S5a), with postnatal follow-up from birth to weaning.
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity studies should include prenatal and postnatal developmental assessment. The gestating animal should be exposed from implantation to closure of the hard palate/end of gestation (stages C, D, E per ICH S5a), with postnatal follow-up from birth to weaning.

rules.rule_text: Developmental toxicity studies must assess maternal antibody transfer to offspring; the maximal human dose should be used if possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: Developmental toxicity studies should assess maternal antibody transfer to offspring. The maximal human dose should be used if possible.

rules.rule_text: Genotoxicity and carcinogenicity studies are normally NOT required for the final vaccine formulation; they may be required for novel adjuvants or additives not previously used in licensed vaccines.
rules.verbatim_quote: Genotoxicity and carcinogenicity studies are normally not required for the final vaccine formulation. They may be required for novel adjuvants or additives not previously used in licensed vaccines.
rules.exceptions: Novel adjuvants and additives may require genotoxicity testing; carcinogenicity is NOT required for vaccine antigens

rules.rule_text: In vitro genotoxicity tests for novel adjuvants or additives must be performed prior to first human exposure; a full battery of genotoxicity tests may be performed in parallel with clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: In vitro genotoxicity tests for novel adjuvants or additives should be performed prior to first human exposure. A full battery of genotoxicity tests may be performed in parallel with clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Safety pharmacology studies are not routinely required for vaccines; they should be conducted only if data suggest effects on vital functions beyond the immune system, and may be incorporated into the toxicity assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety pharmacology studies are not routinely required. They should be conducted if data suggest effects on vital functions beyond the immune system, and may be incorporated into the toxicity assessment.
rules.exceptions: Required only when there is evidence of effects on vital functions other than the immune system

rules.rule_text: Pharmacokinetic studies are normally NOT required for vaccines; they may be needed on a case-by-case basis for novel adjuvants or alternative routes of administration; distribution studies are required for new formulations, novel adjuvants, or alternative routes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacokinetic studies are normally not needed for vaccines. They may be needed on a case-by-case basis for novel adjuvants or alternative routes. Distribution studies are required for new formulations, novel adjuvants, or alternative routes.
rules.exceptions: Standard vaccines do not require pharmacokinetic studies

rules.rule_text: Adjuvants that comply with pharmacopoeial monographs may use those standards; toxicology of a novel adjuvant alone must be assessed first before testing in combination with antigens.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adjuvants complying with pharmacopoeial monographs may use those standards. The toxicology of a novel adjuvant alone should be assessed first before testing in combination with antigens.
rules.exceptions: Pharmacopoeial compliance is acceptable for established adjuvants

rules.rule_text: Adjuvant activity is not extrapolatable between antigens; safety assessment of the adjuvant must be performed in combination with the specific formulation proposed for clinical use.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adjuvant activity is not extrapolatable between antigens. Safety assessment of the adjuvant should be performed in combination with the formulation proposed for clinical use.

rules.rule_text: No adjuvant is licensed in its own right; compatibility of the adjuvant with all antigens in a combination formulation must be documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: No adjuvant is licensed in its own right. Compatibility of the adjuvant with all antigens in a combination formulation should be documented.

rules.rule_text: For novel additives, excipients, or preservatives, toxicology must be assessed alone first; compatibility with all antigens must be documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: For novel additives, excipients, or preservatives, toxicology should be assessed alone first. Compatibility with all antigens should be documented.
rules.exceptions: Established excipients with pharmacopoeial monographs may not require standalone toxicology

rules.rule_text: The vaccine formulation and delivery device used in nonclinical studies should be identical to that intended for clinical use; pilot studies should be conducted to define conditions for drug delivery in the animal model.
rules.verbatim_quote: The vaccine formulation and delivery device used in nonclinical studies should be identical to that intended for clinical use. Pilot studies should be conducted to define conditions for drug delivery in the animal model.

rules.rule_text: For alternative routes of administration, nonclinical safety studies using the vaccine formulation with or without adjuvant alone in a suitable animal model are required, including dose-finding studies and additional route-specific endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: For alternative routes of administration, nonclinical safety studies using the vaccine formulation with or without adjuvant alone in a suitable animal model are required. Dose-finding studies and additional route-specific endpoints should be included.

rules.rule_text: For mucosal routes of immunization, immunogenicity assessment must include T cell responses, antibody-secreting cells, and cytokine production in addition to standard serological assays.
rules.verbatim_quote: For mucosal routes of immunization, immunogenicity assessment should include T cell responses, antibody-secreting cells, and cytokine production in addition to serological assays.

rules.rule_text: For live attenuated vaccines, attenuation stability markers must be established and documented, with methods to distinguish the vaccine strain from virulent wild-type strains.
rules.verbatim_quote: Attenuation stability markers should be established and documented, with methods to distinguish the vaccine strain from virulent wild-type strains.

rules.rule_text: For live attenuated vaccines derived from neurotropic organisms or where neural tissue was used in attenuation, a neurovirulence test is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: For live attenuated vaccines derived from neurotropic organisms or where neural tissue was used in attenuation, a neurovirulence test is required.
rules.exceptions: Only required when neurotropism or neural tissue is relevant to the vaccine

rules.rule_text: For GMO-based live vaccines, an environmental risk assessment must be performed; shedding assessment and assessment of genetic exchange with non-vaccine strains are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: For GMO-based live vaccines, an environmental risk assessment should be performed. Shedding assessment and assessment of genetic exchange with non-vaccine strains are required.
rules.exceptions: Applies to GMO-based live attenuated vaccines only

rules.rule_text: For combined vaccines, immunogenicity in an animal model must be demonstrated before human trials; compatibility of components must be examined, and new combinations must be compared against individual antigens.
rules.verbatim_quote: For combined vaccines, immunogenicity in an animal model should be demonstrated before human trials. Compatibility of components should be examined. New combinations should be compared against individual antigens.

rules.rule_text: For combined vaccines, safety evaluation must be conducted on a case-by-case basis if there is a specific toxicity concern related to the combination.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety evaluation for combined vaccines should be conducted on a case-by-case basis if there is a specific toxicity concern.
rules.exceptions: Not required unless a specific toxicity concern is identified

rules.rule_text: The histopathological examination in repeated-dose toxicity studies must cover a comprehensive tissue list including approximately 40 tissues; immune organs (spleen, thymus, lymph nodes) and pivotal organs must always be examined.
rules.source_section: Appendix: List of tissues for repeated dose toxicity study
rules.verbatim_quote: Histopathological examination should cover a comprehensive tissue list including immune organs (spleen, thymus, lymph nodes) and pivotal organs.

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical study reports must include a protocol/study plan approved before study initiation; retrospective protocol approval is not acceptable for pivotal toxicity studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: A protocol or study plan should be approved before study initiation. Retrospective protocol approval is not acceptable for pivotal studies.

rules.rule_text: Validation of nonclinical lot release tests must be completed prior to licensure; tests used during clinical development must be validated before pivotal efficacy or safety trials.
rules.source_section: 2.5 Batch release and independent laboratory evaluation
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation of lot release tests should be finalized prior to licensure. Tests used during clinical development should be validated before pivotal trials.

rules.rule_text: For primary vaccination schedules, the nonclinical toxicity study must include the number of doses at least equal to the number planned for primary vaccination in humans.
rules.verbatim_quote: Doses should be at least equal to the number in humans for primary vaccination.

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical evaluation must be completed to the level required to support clinical trials at the scale intended; for early clinical trials in healthy adults, a basic toxicity package may suffice.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonclinical evaluation should be completed to the level required to support clinical trials at the intended scale. For early clinical trials in healthy adults, a basic toxicity package may suffice.
rules.exceptions: Reduced packages for early-phase trials in healthy adults
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on clinical evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Carcinogenicity studies are NOT required for vaccine antigens; the requirement applies only to novel adjuvants or additives where genotoxicity data indicate a potential signal.
rules.verbatim_quote: Carcinogenicity is not required for vaccine antigens. The requirement applies only to novel adjuvants or additives where genotoxicity data indicate a potential signal.
rules.exceptions: Vaccine antigens are fully exempt from carcinogenicity requirements

rules.rule_text: Seroconversion must be assessed as an endpoint in immunogenicity studies; a positive seroconversion response in the animal model is required to validate the model's relevance before clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Seroconversion should be assessed as an endpoint in immunogenicity studies. A positive seroconversion response is required to validate the animal model before clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: For novel vaccine products, pilot studies must be conducted to define conditions for drug delivery in the animal model prior to initiating pivotal nonclinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pilot studies should be conducted to define conditions for drug delivery in the animal model prior to initiating pivotal nonclinical studies.
rules.exceptions: Applies to novel vaccine platforms and delivery devices
